current_finding.title: Missing email allowlist validation for gallery access
current_finding.description: The gallery authentication endpoint (/auth/view) does not validate client emails against a per-gallery allowlist or domain-based access control. Currently, any syntactically valid email receives a JWT token for any gallery, which effectively grants unauthenticated access to galleries. This is a significant authorization bypass vulnerability.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Implement email allowlist validation: (1) Add 'allowed_emails' and/or 'allowed_domains' fields to the gallery DynamoDB item, (2) In handle_view_auth(), after validating the gallery exists, check if the provided email is in the allowlist before issuing a JWT, (3) Return 403 Forbidden if email is not authorized.

current_finding.title: No JWT revocation mechanism for gallery admin tokens
current_finding.description: Gallery admin JWTs are issued with long TTLs and have no revocation mechanism. If an admin account is compromised or an admin is removed, their token remains valid until expiration. The code acknowledges this limitation (lines 1024-1027 in admin/handler.py) and has reduced the TTL from 30 to 7 days as a temporary mitigation, but this is not a proper solution.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Implement token revocation: (1) Add a token revocation check in authenticate() function before accepting a JWT, (2) Option A: Implement a revocation table that tracks invalidated token JTI claims with expiration, or (3) Option B: Use short-lived access tokens (15 min) with a refresh token flow. The code already has CAT-022 comment about re-validating account status (line 406) - extend this to also check a revocation list.

current_finding.title: Duplicate uuid import in auth_view handler
current_finding.description: The uuid module is imported twice in lambda/auth_view/handler.py - once on line 9 and again on line 25. This creates a redefined name (F811 linter warning) and indicates disorganized imports.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Remove the duplicate import on line 25 and consolidate all imports at the top of the file in the standard order: stdlib imports first, then third-party imports, then local imports.

current_finding.title: Demo key validation uses unbounded table scan without pagination limit
current_finding.description: The _validate_demo_key() function in admin/handler.py uses _scan_all() to retrieve demo sessions, which scans the entire table. The code includes a comment explaining why Limit=1 cannot be used (lines 361-362), but the current implementation could be inefficient if the demo_sessions table grows large.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add a Global Secondary Index (GSI) on the demo_sessions table with demo_key as the partition key. Then use query() instead of scan(). This would reduce the operation from O(n) to O(1) or O(log n). Alternatively, use FilterExpression with a Limit on the number of pages returned (e.g., limit to 10 items scanned before returning).

current_finding.title: Very short gallery listings cache TTL (2 seconds) may impact performance
current_finding.description: The public gallery listings cache in auth_view/handler.py uses a 2-second TTL (line 519) to prevent cross-tenant leaks in multi-origin setups. While the conservative TTL is understandable for security, it may cause excessive DynamoDB scans under high concurrency.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Consider increasing the cache TTL to 5-10 seconds and evaluating whether cross-tenant leaks are actually a risk in the deployment environment. If multi-origin setup is indeed a risk, consider caching per-origin or per-request-context instead of globally. Monitor DynamoDB scan metrics to validate the impact.

current_finding.title: Email validation regex patterns are inconsistent across codebase
current_finding.description: Email validation is performed using different regex patterns in different files, which could lead to inconsistent validation logic and potential security issues.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Define a single canonical email validation regex at module level (or in a shared constants file) and use it consistently across all handlers. The admin/handler.py pattern is more restrictive and preferred. Create a utility function email_is_valid(email) in each handler or import from a shared module.

current_finding.title: Bare exception handling in JWT verification could hide real errors
current_finding.description: Multiple places catch broad exceptions without specific error handling, particularly in JWT verification and secret loading. This can hide real errors and make debugging difficult.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Catch specific exceptions: (1) ValueError for JSON decode errors, (2) KeyError for missing JWT fields, (3) TypeError for type mismatches. Add logging for unexpected exceptions before returning None so errors can be diagnosed in production.

current_finding.title: Unused imports in test files (F401)
current_finding.description: Two test files have unused imports that should be removed: tests/unit/test_zip_privacy.py imports BytesIO and MagicMock but doesn't use them.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Remove the unused imports: remove BytesIO from line 5, and remove MagicMock from line 6 (keep patch).

current_finding.title: Unsorted import blocks in test files (I001)
current_finding.description: Five test files have unsorted or unformatted import blocks, triggering ruff I001 warnings. These should be organized according to isort/ruff conventions.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Run 'ruff format tests/' to auto-fix import ordering in all test files. Or manually reorganize imports to group stdlib, then third-party, then local imports alphabetically within each group.

current_finding.title: Potential cache collision risk in globals without clear expiration semantics
current_finding.description: Multiple handlers use module-level caches (e.g., _jwt_secret_cache, _galleries_response_cache) without clear semantics about what happens when cache expires. The cache expiration logic is duplicated across files.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Consider extracting cache management into a helper utility class with consistent expiration semantics, or use a simple cache decorator. Alternatively, document the cache invalidation strategy clearly in comments.

current_finding.title: Magic numbers scattered throughout handlers without clear semantics
current_finding.description: Various magic numbers are used throughout the handlers without clear explanation (e.g., bcrypt rounds=12, timeout values, cache TTLs, max attempts). These should be documented or centralized.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add comments explaining why specific values were chosen. Group related constants (e.g., all timeout values) and document in a constants section at the top of each handler.

current_finding.title: 40 linter errors - unused imports and variables
current_finding.description: Ruff linter detected 40 errors across the codebase. Most are unused imports (F401) and unused variables (F841). 35 errors are automatically fixable with ruff --fix.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Run 'python3 -m ruff check . --fix' to automatically remove unused imports and variables. Manually rename ambiguous variable names like 'l' to clearer names like 'lane'.

current_finding.title: Broad exception handling masks real errors
current_finding.file: backoffice/regression.py, backoffice/server.py, backoffice/sync/engine.py, backoffice/sync/providers/aws.py, backoffice/setup.py, tests/test_servers.py
current_finding.description: 6 files use broad 'except Exception:' or bare 'except:' clauses that catch all exceptions including SystemExit and KeyboardInterrupt, making debugging difficult.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Replace broad except clauses with specific exception types:
- Instead of 'except Exception:', use 'except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError)'
- Use 'except FileNotFoundError:' instead of bare except
- Add explicit re-raise for unexpected exceptions

current_finding.title: Use of shell=True in subprocess.run()
current_finding.description: backoffice/regression.py line 96 uses subprocess.run() with shell=True. While commands come from config file, this is a security anti-pattern vulnerable to injection if config format changes.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Use list-based command passing instead of shell=True. Replace:
subprocess.run('python3 -m pytest --cov=.', shell=True)
With:
subprocess.run(['python3', '-m', 'pytest', '--cov', '.'], shell=False)
For dynamic commands, use shlex.split() to safely tokenize.

current_finding.title: Potential CORS logic issue in api_server.py
current_finding.description: The CORS header logic has a subtle issue: if '*' is in allowed_origins, the code echoes back the origin without validating it, defeating CORS protection.

current_finding.title: Missing error handling for subprocess calls
current_finding.description: Multiple subprocess.run() calls in api_server.py and server.py do not check return codes. Agent script failures are silently ignored.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Check return codes and log failures:
result = subprocess.run(args, cwd=str(r), capture_output=True)
if result.returncode != 0:
    logger.error(f"Agent {dept} failed: {result.stderr}")

current_finding.title: Race condition in running_jobs tracking
current_finding.description: The running_jobs dict is checked under lock, but the thread starts after lock is released, creating a window for duplicate job starts.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Start the thread inside the lock:
with running_lock:
    if dept in running_jobs:
        return False
    t = threading.Thread(...)
    running_jobs[dept] = t
    t.start()  # <-- Inside lock

current_finding.title: Ambiguous variable names in tests
current_finding.description: tests/test_delivery.py uses 'l' (lowercase letter L) as loop variable in list comprehensions. This is ambiguous with '1' and violates PEP 8.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Rename 'l' to 'lane':
sprint_now = next(lane for lane in result['sprint_lanes'] if lane['lane'] == 'Sprint Now')

current_finding.title: Unnecessary environment dict copy in subprocess
current_finding.description: backoffice/regression.py line 100 uses {**os.environ} creating an unnecessary dict copy. subprocess.run() uses os.environ by default.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Remove the env parameter or pass None:
Before: subprocess.run(..., env={**os.environ})
After: subprocess.run(...)
Or: subprocess.run(..., env=None)

current_finding.title: Some functions lack comprehensive docstrings
current_finding.description: Some public functions in aggregate.py and sync/engine.py have minimal docstrings without parameter or return documentation.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add comprehensive docstrings with parameter and return type documentation following Google or NumPy format.

current_finding.title: Local data is not scoped per signed-in user, so one user's private data can leak into another account
current_finding.description: The local persistence layer uses global keys like `selah_studies`, `selah_journals`, and `selah_bookmarks` for every browser session instead of namespacing by user ID. Signing out only clears the auth token and cached encryption material, so the next person who signs in on the same browser inherits the previous user's local data set. The settings page then backs up the entire global export into whichever account is currently signed in.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Namespace all local storage keys by a stable user scope such as authenticated user ID or an anonymous device ID, and migrate existing data. At minimum, clear user-owned local data on logout/account switch and prevent account backup from exporting records created under a different user scope.

current_finding.title: Account backup silently uploads plaintext journals whenever the encryption key is unavailable
current_finding.description: The backup path only encrypts journal fields if `masterKey` is present. The settings page always allows account backup and passes `masterKey` as-is, including `null`. That means any signed-in session that has lost or not unlocked its in-memory key will upload raw journal content to the server even though the product claims journal entries are zero-knowledge encrypted and unreadable by the server.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Fail closed: block account backup until encryption is ready for any journal export, prompt the user to unlock first, and never send plaintext journal content to the API from this backup path.

current_finding.title: Encrypted journals are written to DynamoDB but the read API drops the encrypted payload
current_finding.description: The journal write handler persists `_encrypted`, `_encVersion`, and `_payload` for encrypted entries, but the read handler only maps plaintext fields like `content`, `prompts`, and `tags`. For encrypted rows, those fields are absent, so the GET response omits the only recoverable journal body. This makes server-backed journal recovery and multi-device sync impossible for encrypted entries.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Return `_encrypted`, `_encVersion`, `_payload`, and preserved metadata for encrypted entries, and add an integration test that saves an encrypted journal and then reads back the exact encrypted blob.

current_finding.title: Multiple DynamoDB list endpoints read only the first page and silently truncate larger accounts
current_finding.description: User-facing read handlers for studies, journals, bookmarks, daily verses, and stats issue a single `QueryCommand` and never follow `LastEvaluatedKey`. DynamoDB caps a query page at roughly 1 MB, so once an account grows beyond that size the API will start returning partial datasets and incorrect counts without any error. The account deletion path in the same file already demonstrates the correct pagination loop, which these handlers do not use.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Extract a reusable paginated query helper that loops on `LastEvaluatedKey`, and use it for all collection reads and count computations. For stats, prefer `Select: 'COUNT'` with pagination or precomputed counters.

current_finding.title: Password reset and email verification codes are stored in plaintext
current_finding.description: The API writes six-digit reset and verification codes directly into DynamoDB and later compares them as plaintext. Anyone with read access to the table, backups, or debugging exports can use live codes to reset passwords or verify accounts during their validity window. These secrets should be treated like authentication tokens and stored only as hashes.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Hash reset and verification codes before storage, compare hashes in constant time, and keep only metadata like expiry and attempt counts in plaintext.

current_finding.title: h3 dependency has multiple path traversal and injection vulnerabilities
current_finding.description: The h3 package (version <=1.15.8) has been identified with multiple high-severity vulnerabilities including path traversal via percent-encoded dot segments, SSE event injection, and double decoding bypass attacks. These vulnerabilities could allow arbitrary file reads and event stream injection attacks.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Run 'npm audit fix' to update h3 to a patched version. Ensure all devDependencies are updated to their latest secure versions.

current_finding.title: devalue dependency has prototype pollution vulnerability
current_finding.description: The devalue package (version <=5.6.3) contains a prototype pollution vulnerability in devalue.parse and devalue.unflatten functions. This vulnerability allows emission of objects with __proto__ own properties, which could lead to unintended property pollution.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Run 'npm audit fix' to update devalue to a patched version that addresses the prototype pollution vulnerability.

current_finding.title: lodash dependency has prototype pollution in _.unset and _.omit
current_finding.description: The lodash package (versions 4.0.0 - 4.17.21) contains a prototype pollution vulnerability in the _.unset and _.omit functions. This vulnerability is present as a transitive dependency through yaml-language-server → @astrojs/language-server.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Run 'npm audit fix' to update lodash and its dependents to patched versions. May require updating @astrojs/check or other Astro tooling.

current_finding.title: Content Security Policy uses unsafe-inline for script-src
current_finding.description: The Base.astro layout includes a Content-Security-Policy meta tag that allows unsafe-inline for script-src. While this is necessary for the inline Plausible analytics script, it weakens the overall XSS protection provided by CSP.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Consider using a nonce-based CSP instead of unsafe-inline. Update the Plausible script initialization to use a nonce attribute. The current implementation is acceptable for a static site, but nonce-based CSP would be more secure.

current_finding.title: Email validation uses basic regex pattern
current_finding.description: The contact form email validation uses a simple regex pattern for client-side validation. While this pattern catches obvious invalid emails, it does not strictly conform to RFC 5322 email format specifications.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: The current regex is acceptable for client-side validation. Server-side validation on the AWS Lambda endpoint is what truly matters for security.

current_finding.title: Parallax effect animation may impact performance on low-end devices
current_finding.description: The index.astro implements a custom parallax effect using requestAnimationFrame with scroll listeners. While implemented with throttling, this could still impact performance on older devices.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: The current implementation with requestAnimationFrame and passive listeners is already optimized. This is acceptable as-is.

current_finding.title: Bare except clause catching all exceptions without logging
current_finding.description: Multiple exception handlers use bare `except Exception:` without logging errors, which silently hides failures and makes debugging difficult. This masks real errors and security issues.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add logging for all exception handlers: `except Exception as e: logger.error(f'License secret check failed: {e}')`

current_finding.title: Password stored in plaintext in environment before Secret Manager write
current_finding.description: Console password is passed through environment variable `console_password` in request body and temporarily stored in plaintext in Python memory/Cloud Build substitutions before being written to Secret Manager. The password string is also visible in audit logs and build metadata.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
1) Remove password from metadata completely
2) Never log or include password in build substitutions
3) Store only in Secret Manager immediately
4) Use `bytes` type to prevent accidental string conversion in logs

current_finding.title: Multiple bare except clauses swallowing all exceptions
current_finding.description: Eight functions use bare `except Exception:` without any logging or error propagation. This includes critical paths like passphrase loading (_get_passphrase line 563), Google token verification (line 578), and quota capacity checks (line 649).
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add logging: `except Exception as e: app.logger.warning(f'Auth secret load failed: {e}'); return ''`

current_finding.title: Inadequate regex validation for GCP Project IDs
current_finding.description: The PROJECT_RE regex does not enforce strict GCP project ID requirements. GCP project IDs cannot contain consecutive hyphens or end with hyphens. The validation is too permissive.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Validate against GCP API requirements or use stricter regex that checks for consecutive/trailing hyphens

current_finding.title: Recursion in classification script without depth limit
current_finding.description: The `removesubgroups()` and `addsubgroups()` recursive functions have no depth limit or cycle detection. A circular parent-child relationship in the JSON data would cause infinite recursion and stack overflow.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add recursion depth limit and visited set for cycle detection

current_finding.title: No error handling for file I/O in classification transformation
current_finding.description: The Python classification transformation script opens files but does not handle FileNotFoundError, IOError, or JSONDecodeError. If a backup file is missing or corrupted, the script crashes.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add try-except around file operations with clear error messages

current_finding.title: Global mutable variable for passphrase caching not thread-safe
current_finding.description: The `_cached_passphrase` global variable is modified without locks. In a Flask app with concurrent requests, this could lead to race conditions where multiple threads read/write the cached value simultaneously.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Use functools.lru_cache or thread-safe cache mechanism

current_finding.title: No null/None safety checks in Terraform state file parsing
current_finding.description: The `read_metadata()` and `read_outputs()` functions assume JSON structure without validating that required keys exist. If GCS returns malformed JSON, the code could crash with KeyError.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Use meta.get() with defaults instead of direct key access

current_finding.title: Insecure passphrase comparison timing vulnerability
current_finding.description: The passphrase verification uses simple string equality (`token == passphrase`), which is vulnerable to timing attacks. An attacker could measure response time to brute-force the passphrase.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Use secrets.compare_digest() for timing-safe comparison

current_finding.title: Weak Random Number Generation for Verification Codes
current_finding.description: The Lambda API handler uses Math.random() to generate verification codes used for email verification, password reset, and other critical security operations. Math.random() is cryptographically weak and predictable, making verification codes vulnerable to brute force attacks.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Replace Math.random() with crypto.randomBytes() for generating verification codes. Example: `const verifyCode = crypto.randomBytes(3).toString('hex').toUpperCase();` (generates 6-char hex codes). The crypto module is already imported.

current_finding.title: Known Vulnerabilities in fast-xml-parser Dependency
current_finding.description: The project has 2 moderate severity vulnerabilities in the fast-xml-parser dependency (versions 4.0.0-beta.3 through 5.5.6). The vulnerability is: 'Entity Expansion Limits Bypassed When Set to Zero Due to JavaScript Falsy Evaluation in fast-xml-parser'. This is indirectly used via @aws-sdk/xml-builder.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Run `npm audit fix` to update fast-xml-parser to a patched version. The override in package.json (line 56) already specifies `fast-xml-parser >= 5.5.6`, but the constraint may be too loose. Update to the latest version and verify AWS SDK dependencies are compatible.

current_finding.title: Encryption Keys Cached in localStorage
current_finding.description: Encryption keys (containing sensitive cryptographic material) are cached in localStorage for 'unlock-on-refresh' functionality. While the master key is stored in memory only, the encryption key material stored in localStorage could be vulnerable if the browser's storage is compromised.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Consider using sessionStorage instead of localStorage to limit the lifetime of cached keys to the current session. Alternatively, implement a timeout mechanism that clears cached keys after 30 minutes of inactivity. Document this as a known limitation and advise users to re-enter their password after closing and reopening the browser for maximum security.

current_finding.title: Weak ID Generation in Client-Side Storage
current_finding.description: The storage.ts file uses Math.random() to generate IDs for client-side data. While this is not as critical as security-sensitive operations, it could lead to ID collisions in edge cases.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Use `crypto.getRandomValues()` for the random portion or use a UUID library. Example: `return `${Date.now()}-${crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(4)).reduce((a,b) => a + b.toString(16), '')}`

current_finding.title: Unoptimized Image in Contact Page
current_finding.description: The contact page uses a standard HTML tag instead of Next.js Image component for the founder photo. This results in potential LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) impact and missed optimization opportunities.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Replace with Next.js Image component: `import Image from 'next/image'; ... <Image src="/cody-jo-eflin.jpg" alt="Cody Jo Eflin" className="h-auto w-full object-cover" width={400} height={500} priority />`

current_finding.title: Incomplete TODOs in Lambda Code
current_finding.description: The Lambda API handler contains TODO comments for incomplete features that are not yet wired up.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Complete the web-push and Polly implementations when ready. These are intentionally incomplete and documented.

current_finding.title: Weak Random Number Generator for Password Reset Codes
current_finding.description: The password reset code generation uses Math.random() instead of cryptographically secure random values. The generateCode() function generates 6-digit codes using Math.floor(100000 + Math.random() * 900000), which produces only 900,000 possible values. An attacker could brute force a 6-digit code in minutes.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Replace with cryptographically secure random using crypto.getRandomValues(). Generate an 8-character alphanumeric code or longer numeric code with sufficient entropy.

current_finding.title: Multiple Critical Next.js Security Vulnerabilities in Dependencies
current_finding.description: npm audit identified 3 moderate severity vulnerabilities affecting Next.js 16.1.6: (1) HTTP request smuggling in rewrites (GHSA-ggv3-7p47-pfv8), (2) Unbounded next/image disk cache growth exhausting storage (GHSA-3x4c-7xq6-9pq8), (3) Unbounded postponed resume buffering DoS (GHSA-h27x-g6w4-24gq), (4) null origin CSRF bypass in Server Actions (GHSA-mq59-m269-xvcx), (5) null origin dev HMR CSRF bypass (GHSA-jcc7-9wpm-mj36).
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Update Next.js to 16.2.1 or later: npm audit fix --force. Verify CSRF protection uses Origin/Referer headers, not null origin trust.

current_finding.title: XML Entity Expansion Vulnerability in fast-xml-parser Dependency
current_finding.description: Transitive dependency fast-xml-parser 4.0.0-5.5.6 (via @aws-sdk/xml-builder) has vulnerability GHSA-jp2q-39xq-3w4g where entity expansion limits are bypassed when set to zero due to falsy evaluation. This enables billion-laughs DoS attacks.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Run npm audit fix to update @aws-sdk/xml-builder. Ensure entity expansion is explicitly limited (non-zero) when parsing XML.

current_finding.title: Internal Navigation Using <a> Instead of <Link> Component
current_finding.description: src/app/layout.tsx lines 98-100 use <a> elements for internal navigation to /cards/, /guides/, and /blog/ instead of Next.js <Link /> component. This is an ESLint error (@next/next/no-html-link-for-pages).
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Replace with Next.js Link: import Link from 'next/link'; then <Link href="/path">Text</Link> for each of the 3 links.

current_finding.title: User Images Using Instead of Optimized next/image
current_finding.description: src/app/journal/entry/page.tsx line 442 and src/app/journal/view/page.tsx use elements instead of Next.js for user-uploaded journal attachments. ESLint warning @next/next/no-img-element.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: For user-uploaded images, either: (1) Use next/image with fill or fixed sizes, ensuring proper alt text and sizes are set; OR (2) Keep but suppress ESLint warning if user uploads don't justify optimization overhead.

current_finding.title: Missing useEffect Dependencies: Stale Closures
current_finding.description: Two React Hook violations: src/app/history/page.tsx line 95 (useEffect missing masterKey dependency) and src/app/reading/page.tsx line 525 (useEffect missing refreshSavedReadings). Can cause stale state in closures.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add missing dependencies to useEffect arrays: [masterKey] for history/page.tsx, [refreshSavedReadings] for reading/page.tsx.

current_finding.title: Multiple Unused Variables and Imports
current_finding.description: Scattered unused variables: lambda/api/admin.test.mjs (_origin), src/__tests__/auth-context.test.tsx (ReactNode), src/app/insights/page.tsx (CardMoodCorrelation), src/app/learn/page.tsx (useEffect, AuthGuard), src/app/library/page.tsx (AuthGuard), src/app/recap/page.tsx (i), src/lib/api.ts (content, prompts, mood, tags).
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Remove unused variables. If intentionally left for future use or to match a pattern, prefix with _ to silence linter.

current_finding.title: Image Placeholder Missing Alt Text
current_finding.description: src/app/journal/entry/page.tsx line 445 renders a fallback <Image> icon when uploaded image fails to load, but parent div has no alt text or aria-label. Violates WCAG 2.1 AA.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add aria-label to parent div: aria-label="Image failed to load" or wrap in a semantic <figure> with <figcaption>.

current_finding.title: DynamoDB Full Table Scan in Admin List Users
current_finding.description: lambda/api/admin.mjs handleListUsers performs a Scan operation without proper indexing or filtering, scanning the entire DynamoDB table on every admin list request. No use of GSI for efficient queries.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Use GSI1 (email-based) with Query instead of Scan. Implement proper cursor-based pagination with ExclusiveStartKey.

current_finding.title: Console Errors Logged Without PII Filtering
current_finding.description: Multiple console.error() calls throughout src/ and lambda/ (history/page.tsx, daily/page.tsx, journal/page.tsx, etc.) log error details to console/CloudWatch without sanitization. Could leak sensitive data in stack traces.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Log only err.message, not full err objects. Use structured logging and filter PII before logging. Consider using a logging service with automatic PII masking.

current_finding.title: Encryption Key Handling: No Runtime Guard Against Accidental Logging
current_finding.description: src/lib/journal-crypto.ts exports toBase64/fromBase64 functions used throughout encryption. No runtime assertions prevent accidental logging of decrypted content if these functions are called with plaintext data.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add optional JSDoc warnings and dev-time assertions that toBase64 is never called with plaintext. Or seal functions in closure to prevent external misuse.

current_finding.title: Generic OG Image Alt Text Across All Pages
current_finding.description: All pages use the same generic alt text for Open Graph images: '${site.productName} social preview'. This generic text doesn't describe the specific content of each page, reducing the effectiveness of social sharing and missing opportunities for contextual information in social media platforms and apps that display OG metadata.

current_finding.title: Missing JSON-LD Schema on Gallery Frontend Pages
current_finding.description: The frontend gallery application (frontend/gallery.html, frontend/accessibility.html, etc.) has basic meta tags (title, description, OG tags) but no JSON-LD structured data. This prevents search engines and AI models from understanding the semantic structure of gallery pages, missing opportunities for rich snippets and AI discoverability.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Add JSON-LD WebPage schema to frontend/gallery.html after the Twitter Card meta tags:

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "WebPage",
  "name": "Client Photo Gallery",
  "description": "View and download your photo gallery. Powered by Analogify Studio and ChromaHaus automated image processing.",
  "url": "https://galleries.analogifystudio.com/gallery.html",
  "publisher": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Analogify Studio",
    "logo": "https://analogifystudio.com/og-default.svg"
  },
  "about": {
    "@type": "Thing",
    "name": "Photo Gallery",
    "description": "Secure, branded photo gallery with selective downloads and moderated comments"
  },
  "isAccessibleForFree": false
}
</script>

current_finding.title: FAQ Schema Missing on Zen FAQ Section
current_finding.description: The Analogify Zen page (zen.astro, lines 218-240) has a well-structured FAQ section with questions and answers, but it lacks FAQPage and Question schema markup. This misses the opportunity for featured snippets in search results and voice assistant integration.

current_finding.title: Inconsistent Meta Description Length
current_finding.description: Meta descriptions vary significantly in length. Most are 120-160 characters (optimal), but some are shorter, like accessibility.astro at 47 characters. While Google will generate its own if needed, shorter descriptions reduce SERP click-through rate and appear truncated.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Expand meta description to 120-160 characters:

Change from:
description="Accessibility is ongoing engineering work."

To:
description="Learn about Analogify's ongoing commitment to accessibility, WCAG 2.1 AA compliance, and how we build inclusive photo gallery experiences for all photographers."

current_finding.title: Suboptimal Meta Descriptions on Support and Security Pages
current_finding.description: Support and security pages have descriptions under 100 characters. These pages are likely search targets for photographers looking for help or trust signals, and shorter descriptions reduce CTR.

Suggested: "Get help with Analogify Studio workflows, access issues, gallery management, and delivery questions. Response time: 2 business days. Privacy-first support process."

current_finding.title: Missing BreadcrumbList Schema on Multi-Level Content
current_finding.description: For a flat-structure site, breadcrumbs aren't critical, but pages like 'Demo' and 'Features' could benefit from BreadcrumbList schema to show hierarchy in search results (Home > Features or Home > Demo).

current_finding.title: No Image Optimization Indicators on Frontend App
current_finding.description: Frontend gallery application (frontend/gallery.html) likely serves user-uploaded photos but lacks visible optimization patterns like lazy loading attributes, srcset, or WebP format hints. This is likely handled by JavaScript framework, but best practices suggest explicit attributes.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: If frontend gallery serves images dynamically via JavaScript, document the optimization in comments or verify the JS framework is: 1. Using lazy loading (loading="lazy" attribute or equivalent) 2. Serving responsive srcset (multiple resolutions) 3. Using modern formats with fallbacks (WebP/AVIF with JPEG fallback) 4. Setting explicit width/height to prevent layout shift Example pattern for image rendering in generated HTML:

current_finding.title: Consider IndexNow Integration for Faster Indexing
current_finding.description: The site has a valid sitemap and robots.txt, but IndexNow (Microsoft Bing's fast indexing API) is not integrated. This allows instant notification to search engines when new content is published.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Optional future enhancement:
1. Generate IndexNow key from Bing Webmaster Tools
2. Add to public/index-now-key.txt
3. Call IndexNow API on build:

4. Or add to sitemap header in robots.txt for automatic pickup

current_finding.title: Missing robots.txt file
current_finding.description: No robots.txt file exists at the web root. Required to guide search engine crawlers and control indexing behavior.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Create /robots.txt at web root with standard rules for crawlers and sitemap reference.

current_finding.title: Missing sitemap.xml file
current_finding.description: No sitemap.xml exists to list all public pages. Critical for search engine discovery.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Generate sitemap.xml listing all 24 public dashboard pages with lastmod dates and priorities.

current_finding.title: Missing meta descriptions on 22 of 24 dashboard pages
current_finding.description: Only admin.html and self-audit.html have meta descriptions. Missing on all other pages. Meta descriptions appear in search results and affect click-through rates.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add unique 120-160 character meta descriptions to each dashboard HTML file explaining page purpose and content.

current_finding.title: Missing canonical URLs on all dashboard pages
current_finding.description: No canonical link elements found on any dashboard. Canonicals prevent duplicate content issues and consolidate link equity.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add self-referencing canonical links to each dashboard page pointing to correct absolute URLs.

current_finding.title: Missing Open Graph meta tags
current_finding.description: No Open Graph tags found. OG tags enable rich previews when pages are shared on social platforms.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url, og:type to each page. Create 1200x630px OG images.

current_finding.title: Missing Twitter Card meta tags
current_finding.description: No Twitter Card tags found. Enables rich previews on X when links are shared.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add twitter:card, twitter:title, twitter:description, twitter:image meta tags. Use 1200x675px images.

current_finding.title: No structured data (JSON-LD) markup
current_finding.description: No schema.org JSON-LD found on dashboard pages. Important for AI search engines and voice assistants.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add Organization and WebSite schema to base template. Add department-specific schema to relevant pages.

current_finding.title: Inconsistent page title patterns
current_finding.description: Titles follow three different patterns: some with brand, some without. Inconsistency reduces SEO impact.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Standardize all titles to consistent format: [Page Name] — Cody Jo Method (50-60 characters).

current_finding.title: H1 heading structure varies across pages
current_finding.description: H1 placement and structure inconsistent. Best practice is one H1 per page in prominent location.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Ensure every page has exactly one H1 as main heading. Keep semantic structure clean.

current_finding.title: Content rendered by JavaScript not visible to crawlers
current_finding.description: Key dashboard data rendered via JavaScript into empty div containers. AI crawlers may not see dynamically-rendered content.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Consider server-side rendering for key sections or ensure JavaScript content is crawlable.

current_finding.title: Minimal static content optimized for organic search
current_finding.description: Dashboard pages are 95% CSS/JavaScript with little static text. No landing page with marketing copy for organic search.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add landing page with static content explaining Back Office purpose. Ensure documentation pages have crawlable content.

current_finding.title: Missing image alt attributes
current_finding.description: Dynamic images (e.g., product thumbnails) lack alt text. Static images may also lack alt attributes.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add descriptive alt text to all img tags. Use alt='' for decorative images.

current_finding.title: Link handling could be optimized
current_finding.description: Internal links appropriately avoid target='_blank'. External links would benefit from rel attributes.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: For future external links, use rel='noopener noreferrer' for security.

current_finding.title: JavaScript loading could be optimized
current_finding.description: Scripts loaded synchronously in head. Could defer non-critical scripts to improve LCP.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add defer attribute to non-critical scripts or move to end of body.

current_finding.title: Google Fonts loading already optimized
current_finding.description: Fonts already use display=swap for optimal loading. No action needed.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: No action needed. Font loading is properly optimized.

current_finding.title: FAQ schema underutilized
current_finding.description: FAQ page has Q&A content but lacks FAQPage schema markup for AI assistants and voice search.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add FAQPage schema with Question/Answer pairs extracted from faq.html content.

current_finding.title: No breadcrumb schema for navigation
current_finding.description: Pages lack BreadcrumbList schema to indicate navigation hierarchy.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add BreadcrumbList schema to main dashboard pages (optional).

current_finding.title: Product-specific pages lack unique content
current_finding.description: Product dashboards (selah.html, analogify.html, etc.) are duplicates of index.html. No unique descriptions or content.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add unique descriptions and brief product overviews to each dashboard.

current_finding.title: 18 exported routes reuse the homepage title, description, and canonical URL
current_finding.description: The root metadata in the App Router layout defines homepage-level title, description, Open Graph, Twitter, and URL values, but most routes do not override them. In the generated export, 18 route HTML files reuse the same title, the same 148-character meta description, and the same canonical URL pointing to https://selah.codyjo.com/. That collapses page relevance, creates duplicate metadata across the site, and tells search engines that non-home pages canonicalize back to the homepage.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add route-level `metadata` or `generateMetadata()` exports for every public page so each route emits a unique title, description, canonical URL, and OG/Twitter URL. Example: `export const metadata = { title: "Reading Plans", description: "Browse guided Bible reading plans...", alternates: { canonical: "https://selah.codyjo.com/plans/" }, openGraph: { url: "https://selah.codyjo.com/plans/" } }`.

current_finding.title: Books and Study export as client-side placeholders instead of crawlable content
current_finding.description: Two SEO-critical routes, `/books/` and `/study/`, do not ship meaningful HTML in the static export. The generated HTML contains a client-render bailout template and loading copy instead of headings, navigation, or text content. Search engines and AI systems that rely on the initial HTML only see placeholders until JavaScript finishes, which is especially risky for a static-export site.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Move the route shell and default content into server-rendered App Router components, and avoid forcing `useSearchParams()` bailouts for the primary page body. Pre-render a static browse experience with headings and internal links, then hydrate interactive chapter loading on top.

current_finding.title: The sitemap omits five public indexable routes
current_finding.description: The codebase exports 17 page routes from `src/app`, but `public/sitemap.xml` includes only 12 URLs. The missing routes are `/updates`, `/login`, `/register`, `/forgot-password`, and `/reset-password`. All five pages are publicly reachable and currently emit `index, follow` robots tags, so the sitemap is incomplete relative to the live route set.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Generate the sitemap from the route manifest or maintain it from a single source of truth so every public route is emitted automatically. If auth pages should not be indexed, change their robots directives to `noindex` instead of leaving them public but absent from the sitemap.

current_finding.title: The sitemap has no `lastmod` dates for any URL
current_finding.description: The sitemap lists `loc`, `changefreq`, and `priority`, but it never emits `lastmod`. That removes an explicit freshness signal for search engines and makes the static sitemap harder to trust for recently updated routes such as `/updates/` and content-like pages.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add `<lastmod>` for every URL, preferably from build metadata or content timestamps. Example: `<lastmod>2026-03-11</lastmod>`.

current_finding.title: 404 pages send conflicting robots directives and canonicalize to the homepage
current_finding.description: The generated 404 output includes both `noindex` and `index, follow`, and also emits a canonical pointing at the homepage. That creates mixed signals for crawlers: the page says it is a 404, tells bots not to index it, then also inherits the homepage canonical and global metadata.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Override metadata for the not-found route so it emits only `noindex, follow` and no homepage canonical. In Next App Router, set a dedicated `metadata` object for the not-found boundary rather than inheriting the root canonical.

current_finding.title: The updates page is orphaned in crawlable HTML
current_finding.description: The only product navigation link to `/updates` lives inside a menu that renders only when `menuOpen` is true. In the exported HTML, that menu is closed by default, so crawlers do not see a normal internal link to `/updates/`. Combined with the sitemap omission, the updates page has weak discoverability.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add a normal static link to `/updates` in always-rendered navigation, the footer, or the homepage, and include the route in the sitemap.

current_finding.title: The updates feed lacks semantic article/time markup and freshness schema
current_finding.description: The updates page renders change-log entries with generic `div` and `span` elements. It does not use `<article>`, `<time dateTime>`, or `BlogPosting`/`Article` JSON-LD, even though the underlying data includes publication dates and titles. That leaves AI systems with weaker entity, date, and freshness cues.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Render each entry as an `<article>` with a `<time dateTime={update.date}>` element, and add `BlogPosting` JSON-LD containing `headline`, `datePublished`, `dateModified`, and `description`.

current_finding.title: Open Graph and Twitter tags on non-home pages still point to the homepage
current_finding.description: The layout hard-codes homepage Open Graph and Twitter values, and most routes never override them. In the exported HTML, non-home pages such as `/updates/`, `/books/`, `/journal/`, and `/study/` all share the homepage OG title, description, and URL. Shared links therefore preview as the homepage instead of the page being shared.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Set route-specific `openGraph` and `twitter` metadata per page, including page-specific `title`, `description`, and `url`. For example, `/updates/` should emit `og:url=https://selah.codyjo.com/updates/` and its own release-note title/description.

current_finding.title: Built pages do not emit `og:image` or `twitter:image` tags
current_finding.description: Although the root metadata object defines image values, the generated HTML pages do not include `og:image` or `twitter:image`. The build output shows title, description, URL, site name, and card tags only. As a result, shared URLs may render without a predictable preview image.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Verify the metadata build emits image tags for exported routes, preferably using an absolute PNG or JPG URL such as `https://selah.codyjo.com/og.png`, then confirm `og:image` and `twitter:image` are present in the built HTML.

current_finding.title: Primary content on key routes is delayed behind hydration and placeholder UI
current_finding.description: Key routes rely on client-side state and hydration before meaningful content appears. The exported HTML for `/books/`, `/study/`, `/login/`, `/register/`, and `/reset-password/` is a spinner or loading message rather than the final page body, while the document still loads multiple JavaScript chunks up front. That pattern raises the cost of first render and delays meaningful paint.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Server-render the initial headings, descriptive copy, and navigation for critical routes, then hydrate only the interactive controls. That reduces main-thread work and gives crawlers and users useful HTML immediately.

current_finding.title: Noindex Back Office routes are still published in the sitemap
current_finding.description: The generated sitemap includes Back Office admin/demo/dashboard-launch routes that explicitly send `noindex, nofollow`. That creates conflicting crawl signals: the sitemap asks crawlers to discover the URLs while the page-level robots tag asks them not to index them.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Exclude all `noindex` routes from sitemap generation. In Astro, either move those routes out of the public sitemap path, or filter them out when generating the sitemap so only canonical indexable URLs are emitted.

current_finding.title: The public documentation endpoint is missing a canonical URL
current_finding.description: The `/back-office/documentation.html` route is generated as raw HTML instead of using the shared `Base` layout, and it omits a canonical tag entirely.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add `<link rel="canonical" href="https://codyjo.com/back-office/documentation.html">` to the head, or migrate the page into an Astro page that reuses `Base.astro` so canonical generation stays consistent site-wide.

current_finding.title: The public documentation page ships with no JSON-LD or entity schema
current_finding.description: The same `/back-office/documentation.html` endpoint lacks structured data entirely, so search engines and AI systems only see untyped HTML rather than explicit documentation, organization, or article entities.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add JSON-LD for `TechArticle` or `Article` plus publisher/organization metadata, or move the page onto the shared layout and pass a page-specific schema object.

current_finding.title: The public documentation page has no Open Graph or Twitter Card tags
current_finding.description: Because `/back-office/documentation.html` bypasses `Base.astro`, it also misses the site-wide social meta implementation.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:url`, `og:image`, `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, and `twitter:image`, or migrate the page to the shared layout.

current_finding.title: All sitemap lastmod values are set to build time instead of page freshness
current_finding.description: The Astro sitemap integration is configured with `lastmod: new Date()`, so every URL in the sitemap gets the same timestamp from the build rather than an actual content modification date.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Remove the global build-time `lastmod`, or compute per-page dates from content frontmatter or git timestamps so blog posts and static pages emit meaningful freshness data.

current_finding.title: Flagship product pages only expose generic WebSite schema
current_finding.description: Product landing pages such as Analogify, ChromaHaus, Selah, and SEO Privacy Tracker call `Base` without a page-specific schema, so the layout falls back to a generic `WebSite` entity instead of `SoftwareApplication`, `Product`, or another product-specific type.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Pass page-level JSON-LD to each product page using `SoftwareApplication` or `Product` with name, description, URL, applicationCategory, audience, and publisher fields.

current_finding.title: The blog article title template produces titles far beyond recommended length
current_finding.description: Blog post pages append the full product blog name to every article title. That pushes many generated titles into the 85-119 character range, well beyond the requested 50-60 character target.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Shorten the template, for example by dropping the blog suffix for posts or using a shorter brand suffix such as `| Cody Jo Method` only when needed.

current_finding.title: Product blog hub metadata is consistently out of range
current_finding.description: The product blog index template creates short titles and very long descriptions across all six product hubs. Generated descriptions land between 227 and 279 characters, and some titles fall well below the 50-60 character target.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Rewrite the template to produce focused 50-60 character titles and 120-160 character descriptions per product hub, keeping the core keyword and audience but removing the extra explanatory sentence fragments.

current_finding.title: Missing meta description
current_finding.description: The page is missing a meta description tag. While this is an internal tool, a description improves browser tab clarity and API documentation.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add: <meta name="description" content="Configure and deploy Puppet Enterprise demo environments on GCP. Single-page application for infrastructure provisioning.">

current_finding.description: No robots meta tag present. For an authenticated internal tool, should explicitly prevent indexing.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add: <meta name="robots" content="noindex, nofollow, noarchive">

current_finding.description: This form-based interface has no images. This is acceptable for a data entry tool.

current_finding.title: External CSS render-blocking
current_finding.description: Stylesheet is loaded synchronously in head. For a SPA, could improve LCP by inlining critical styles.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Consider: 1) Move CSS to end of body, 2) Use async CSS loading, or 3) Inline critical CSS in head.

current_finding.title: Missing security headers (CSP)
current_finding.description: No Content-Security-Policy meta tag. For authenticated internal tools, CSP improves security.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Add: <meta http-equiv="Content-Security-Policy" content="default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://accounts.google.com/gsi/client; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';">

current_finding.description: Google Sign-In script is loaded asynchronously (good). Consider fetchpriority for auth-critical load.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Consider adding: <script src="..." async defer fetchpriority="high"></script> if this is critical path.

current_finding.title: Links using href='#' with onclick handlers
current_finding.description: Found 3 anchor tags using href='#' with onclick. These don't work without JavaScript.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Use

current_finding.fix_suggestion: Use <button type="button" onclick="..."> instead of <div onclick="...">. Current buttons are correct; ensure all clickable elements are <button> or <a>.

current_finding.title: Page title is concise (potentially too short)
current_finding.description: Title is "PE Demo Bootstrap" (17 chars). Optimal: 50-60 characters for better SERP display.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Change to: <title>PE Demo Bootstrap - Configure Puppet Enterprise on GCP</title>

current_finding.title: Missing metadata on 46 non-marketing pages
current_finding.description: 46 of 67 pages lack exported metadata objects, including critical user-facing pages: /login, /register, /reset-password, /forgot-password, /create, /reader, /study, /journal, /admin, /settings, /quick-start, and all /e/[slug]/* dynamic routes. Without metadata, these pages have missing or default titles, descriptions, and OG tags.

current_finding.title: Sitemap missing 46 pages
current_finding.description: The static sitemap.xml includes only 18 public marketing pages but excludes 46 pages: auth flows (/login, /register, /reset-password), organizer tools (/create, /admin), user utilities (/settings, /reader, /study), and all dynamic event routes (/e/[slug] and children). Sitemaps should include all crawlable pages.

current_finding.title: Missing viewport meta tag explicit declaration
current_finding.description: Root layout.tsx does not explicitly declare a viewport meta tag. While Next.js provides a default viewport, best practice is to explicitly declare it for clarity and SEO crawlers.

current_finding.title: Dynamic pages missing canonical URLs
current_finding.description: Dynamic /e/[slug]/* pages (46 pages total) lack canonical URL declarations. For a static export build, canonical URLs should explicitly point to the published URL to prevent indexing issues with query parameters or protocol mismatches.

current_finding.title: Missing OpenGraph and Twitter card metadata on 46 pages
current_finding.description: 46 pages lack openGraph and twitter metadata objects. Marketing pages (15 pages) have both, but user-facing pages (/login, /create, /settings, /reader, /e/[slug]/*, etc.) default to root layout OG tags instead of page-specific social sharing metadata.

current_finding.title: No structured data for Article, Event, or FAQPage schema
current_finding.description: Codebase includes WebApplication and Organization schema (root layout) but no Article, Event, FAQPage, BreadcrumbList, or Person schema. Event pages and feature pages could benefit from rich snippet markup.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Add structured data to key page types:

1. Event pages (/e/[slug]/): Add Event schema with name, description, startDate, endDate, location, image.
2. Features pages: Add Article schema with headline, description, datePublished, author.
3. FAQ content: Use FAQPage schema if applicable.

Example Event schema for /e/[slug]/:

script type="application/ld+json"
{
  '@context': 'https://schema.org',
  '@type': 'Event',
  name: event.name,
  description: event.description,
  startDate: event.startDate,
  endDate: event.endDate,
  location: { '@type': 'Place', name: event.venue },
  image: event.ogImage,
  url: absoluteUrl(`/e/${event.slug}/`)
}

current_finding.title: Images missing width/height attributes on some components
current_finding.description: Most Next.js Image components have width/height (e.g., directory QR codes: width={80}, height={80}), but the contact page uses a regular tag without width/height attributes: with no dimensions, causing potential Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS).

current_finding.title: No lazy loading attribute on above-fold images
current_finding.description: Images in components use loading="eager" or default to eager (non-lazy) loading. The contact page photo uses loading="eager" even though it's above the fold, which is correct. However, many images below the fold should have loading="lazy" to improve page load performance and LCP.
current_finding.fix_suggestion: Audit all tags and Image components for lazy loading: - Above-fold images (hero, header): loading="eager" or default (no lazy) - Below-fold images (gallery, testimonials): loading="lazy" - Next.js Image: use priority={true} for LCP images, default lazy for others

current_finding.title: Inconsistent heading hierarchy on pricing page
current_finding.description: Pricing page uses single <h1> at line 84 but content flows from context div -> h1 with various <h2> elements and styled text. No H2 headings wrapping major sections (packages, included features, next steps), reducing semantic structure for screen readers and AI models.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Add semantic H2 wrappers for major content sections:

Straightforward pilot packages...

Choose Your Package

{packages.map(pkg =>

{pkg.name}

)}

What's Included in Every Pilot

current_finding.title: Limited content structure optimization for AI extraction
current_finding.description: Homepage and feature pages use prose blocks and feature cards but don't employ clear 'answer upfront' patterns or bullet lists that AI models and featured snippet algorithms prefer. Content layout is marketing-oriented (carousel, gradient sections) rather than extraction-optimized (question-answer, lists, direct answers).
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Add FAQ schema and structured content sections: 1. Add explicit 'What is Cordivent?' answer in first 50 words of homepage 2. Add FAQ section to /features with structured Q&A pairs (Who is it for? What makes it different? How does it work?) 3. Implement FAQPage JSON-LD schema 4. Break feature descriptions into bullet lists with numbered steps (How it works section already exists, make it more extraction-friendly) Example:

Common Questions

  • What is Cordivent?

    Cordivent is...

current_finding.title: OG image is static SVG, no per-page dynamic images
current_finding.description: All pages (root + marketing pages that have OG tags) use the same static og.svg image. Events, user content (gallery), and directory don't have dynamic OG images generated from event data.
current_finding.fix_suggestion:
Implement dynamic OG image generation for event pages:

1. Use library like `@vercel/og` or canvas-based image generation
2. Create event-specific OG image endpoint: /api/og?slug=event-slug
3. Reference in generateMetadata():

images: [{ 
  url: `${SITE_URL_NO_TRAILING_SLASH}/api/og?slug=${event.slug}`,
  width: 1200,
